rows.description: Computus Francisci Webber Rectoris à 2do Die Novombris 1752 ad eundem Diem revolutum 1753.
rows.notes: Page title in Latin across top of page. Handwriting legible though abbreviations and archaic Latin make some words uncertain (e.g. 'revolutum'). No monetary columns associated with this line.
Section header (appears centered/indented). No amounts on this line.
Amount columns and digits are very clear (29 | 5 | 8). Description readable though abbreviated ('pro dimid. ad mich.'). Alignment with vertical ruling lines is unambiguous.
Amounts clearly written (16 | 13 | 4). The place-name text is somewhat abbreviated/uncertain ('Frion' reading may be slightly ambiguous) but the monetary columns are well aligned.
Amount (17 | 15 | 8) is clear. The place name is less certain due to handwriting (I read 'Frosthwan'; other similar readings possible).
Monetary columns (4 | 17 | 7) are well-formed. Description legible though 'Sadler' / punctuation are abbreviations.
Amount (4 | 17 | 7) identical to previous line and clearly written. The surname/place 'molinux' read from handwriting; minor uncertainty on exact spelling but context shows regular entry.
Amounts (1 | 17 | 0) are clearly written and validate. 'Bonsington' and abbreviations legible enough for transcription.
Amount (8 | 0 | 0) is unambiguous. The place-name is faint/abbreviated ('Snom.' might be 'Som.' or similar); reading chosen by visual similarity.
Small amount (1 | 9 | 6) clearly aligned. The leading dash indicates a continuation/sub-item; text is slightly cramped but readable.
Large amount (58 | 0 | 0) is very clear. Place name includes parenthesis '(Penn)'; letters readable though older-style formation.
Amount (30 | 0 | 0) clear. The description is short and legible (remainder/sub-item 'Rest. (Hawtin)').
Amount (0 | 10 | 0) is clear. Parenthetical 'Bush' is faint but consistent with the script; classification as an entry is obvious.
Amount (1 | 1 | 0) legible. 'Ladonhall, Jesus Coll.' phrasing matches what is written; small abbreviations present but clear.
Amount (1 | 2 | 6) clear. Place name uncertain in a letter or two ('Grankflyn' transcribed as seen); context shows it is a parish entry.
Small sub-item indicated by dash. Amount (0 | 13 | 0) is clear and within valid ranges.
Sub-item line with amount (0 | 2 | 6) clearly aligned. Description abbreviated but readable.
Another 'pro Capon' entry; amount (1 | 13 | 4) is clear. Handwriting consistent with prior sub-items.

rows.description: Hart Hall pro An. ad mich
rows.notes: Amount (1 | 6 | 8) readable; 'Hart Hall' interpretion is clear though abbreviation 'An.' may mean 'Anno'. Slight ink smudge near the middle of the description.

rows.description: Ladlands, Conyn, pro An. ad mich
rows.notes: Amount (8 | 6 | 8) is legible. The place-name(s) 'Ladlands, Conyn' are uncertain due to cramped cursive and abbreviation; chosen reading fits letter shapes but alternate spellings possible.
Amount (6 | 13 | 4) clear. The description has an abbreviated/short form ('moriton go') which is somewhat unclear; alignment confirms this amount belongs to this line.
Amount (10 | 0 | 0) is clear. Place-name 'Modsham' read from script; reasonable confidence in reading though older spelling may differ.
Amount (4 | 10 | 0) legible. The place-name is difficult to decipher (transcribed as 'Minikinest'); moderate uncertainty about exact letters but monetary placement is unambiguous.

rows.description: Backgate Emunds pro Dim. ad mich.
rows.notes: Line present and text 'Backgate Emunds pro Dim. ad mich.' is legible, but the corresponding amount is not clearly written on the same line (possible that the small amount printed above/below belongs to another line). I left amounts blank due to ambiguous alignment with the visible numerals.

This label appears to introduce receipts for the present year but has an amount (10 | 0 | 0) written on the same rule. Because a monetary value is present this line is classified as an entry rather than a header.
Amount (0 | 10 | 0) clear. The place-name 'Mynchyvrett' is read from elaborate script and could be spelled differently; moderate uncertainty in text only.
Amount (6 | 13 | 4) clear and validates. Description matches earlier similar entry; year '1753' readable.

rows.description: Modenham pro Anno ad Esth. Nativ. 1752
rows.notes: Large amount (62 | 10 | 0) is legible. The place name and phrase 'ad Esth. Nativ. 1752' are slightly abbreviated and the spelling uncertain; monetary digits are unambiguous.
Amount (31 | 0 | 3) readable though the '3' pence is small. Description 'Exchequer Annuity 1/4 year' is clear; 'Ladyd.' is abbreviated but consistent with the script.
Amount (64 | 2 | 6) clear and within valid ranges. The description is somewhat abbreviated but readable; alignment is unambiguous.
Amount (2 | 15 | 0) legible. Description 'Kidlington Augm. Candl.' is a shortened phrase (augmentation for candles) but clearly indicated in the script.
Section header for miscellaneous receipts. No monetary amounts on the header line.

rows.description: Received of the Administratrix of Dr Edgcumbe By hands of Mr John Edgcumbe the sum of Ninety two Pounds in full for her towards the Discharge of yr Workmens Bills payd by Dr Edgcumbe in the Chapple (the whole
rows.notes: Long paragraph-style receipt. The amount (92 | 0 | 0) is clearly aligned to the right. The textual paragraph is faded in places and broken across lines; I transcribed the first line and part of the continuation as it appears. There is moderate uncertainty in exact punctuation/spacing of the long sentence.

This short line appears to continue the paragraph above ('sum being 184:1:1'). No distinct amount on this specific line; the numeric statement itself likely explanatory rather than a separate monetary receipt. Slight fading; left blank for amounts.

rows.description: Received Dr Dunsamys Fees for Degrees
rows.notes: Amount (4 | 0 | 0) clear and aligned. Description 'Dr Dunsamys' (or Dunsany's) is legible; small uncertainty in spelling of the name but purpose 'Fees for Degrees' is clear.

rows.description: Received of mr. Bonney for the year 1752 in part
rows.notes: Amount (50 | 0 | 0) clear. Name 'mr. Bonney' readable; 'in part' phrase is clear. Good alignment with monetary columns.

rows.description: Received of Mr Bray Library money for mids. 1751
rows.notes: Amount (3 | 12 | 6) is legible. 'Mr Bray' and library reference are readable though abbreviation 'mids.' (midst/midsummer) is shorthand; moderate confidence in text.
Final total at bottom of page, boxed/underlined. Monetary digits (553 | 3 | 6) are very clear and validate correctly. Clearly a page total.
